response.original_title: سلیمان میناسیان، مردی با دوربین فیلمبرداری
response.overview: This film honors Solayman Minassian, a distinguished cinematographer in Iranian cinema. Beginning his career as a film technician and cameraman at Ebrahim Golestan's Studio Golestan in the 1960s, Minassian showcased his skill in Golestan's Brick and Mirror (1964). He also collaborated with Forough Farrokhzad, capturing the evocative imagery of her iconic The House is Black (1962). Additionally, he worked on Golestan's documentaries, including Wave, Coral, Rock, Marlik Hills, and Royal Jewels. In this film, Minassian reflects on his creative collaborations with Golestan and Farrokhzad and shares his profound insights into the art of cinematography. Despite decades in the field, Minassian remains captivated by the intricate magic of capturing images, underscoring his enduring passion for the craft.
response.title: Solayman Minassian, a Man with a Movie Camera
